summary: Comprehensive automated coverage added for Marketplace Dynamic Pricing + supporting endpoints. 45/45 tests pass against the public REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL. Pricing math (base × state × doc × urgency × rating premium), breakdown structure, response envelope, 404s, search/filter/sort/pagination, profile detail, review auth/ownership/validation all verified.

minor.issue: State filter compares license_state against state.upper() exact match. Existing DB has records with license_state='Texas' / 'California' (full names) instead of the ISO code, so a filter like ?state=TX returns nothing for those notaries. Only the seeded 'CA' record is filterable. Recommend normalizing license_state at write-time to 2-letter ISO codes.

Quote breakdown line item for state uses input state_code uppercased, but the price has no dependency on the notary's actual license_state. A notary licensed in FL still gets the NY surcharge if the caller passes state_code=NY. Confirm with PM whether the surcharge should be based on the *ceremony* state (current behaviour) or the *notary* state.

QuoteRequest has no Pydantic validation on state_code length / pattern — empty string '' produces label '  state surcharge' with multiplier 1.0. Consider min_length=2/max_length=2 and Literal[...] for urgency.

action_items: Normalize notary_profiles.license_state to 2-letter ISO codes (existing seed data uses 'Texas'/'California' which breaks ?state=TX/?state=CA filtering).
Add Pydantic validation on QuoteRequest fields (state_code length, urgency Literal, document_type Literal or normalized lookup).
Clarify product intent: state surcharge driven by ceremony state vs notary license state.
critical_code_review_comments: marketplace_routes.py search_notaries: aggregates avg_rating in Python by pulling every review for every notary in a loop (N+1 queries). Will not scale beyond a few hundred notaries. Use a $lookup aggregation pipeline.
Quote endpoint returns rating_premium=1.0 silently when review_count < 5 — the breakdown line correctly explains 'needs 5+ reviews at ≥4.5★', good UX.
marketplace_routes.py uses re.escape on `search` correctly (no regex injection) — good.
delete_review returns 404 both for non-existent and not-owned reviews — fine for privacy, but reviewers cannot distinguish. Acceptable.
test_credentials: Used demo@test.com / Demo123! for auth-gated tests (review create/delete). FL notary not required because an existing CA notary 'John Notary' with hourly_rate=75 was already in DB.
seed_data_creation: No seed needed — 4 approved notaries already exist in DB.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Marketplace pricing fully covered by /app/backend/tests/test_marketplace_pricing.py (45 tests). If new multipliers are added, extend the parametrize blocks. Quote endpoint is PUBLIC by design. Note the license_state normalization issue when adding state-filter tests.
rca of the issue: No bug fixes performed — only test coverage added. The minor issues above are observations from code review, not regressions.
